name: Gluten-Free Bread
recipeInstructions:
1. Spray an 8 x 4 inch loaf pan with nonstick spray. Coat the inside with cornmeal and set aside.
2. Dissolve sugar in warm water in a small bowl. Sprinkle yeast over top. Set aside for 10 minutes or until frothy, stirring well.
3. Combine water and minute tapioca in a saucepan. Bring to a boil. Cook for 1 to 2 minutes or until thickened and clear.
4. In a mixing bowl, stir together bean flour, cornmeal, cornstarch, baking powder, and salt.
5. In a separate bowl, whisk egg whites and milk. Whisk in yeast and tapioca mixture. Stir into dry ingredients, beating until smooth. Set aside for 10 minutes.
6. Turn dough out onto a surface generously dusted with cornstarch. Dust hands with cornstarch and sprinkle a little over the dough. Knead 4 to 5 times and fit into the prepared pan. Brush top with milk and sprinkle with poppy seeds.
7. Bake in a 400°F oven for 45 minutes or until browned and the loaf sounds hollow when tapped. Makes 1 loaf, 16 slices.
